findings.title: Flow mismatch: EX-FR-001 `affects` vs process-step transitions
findings.detail: process-step(s) PS-FR-002 transition into it, but its `affects` omits them.

findings.title: Flow mismatch: EX-FR-002 `affects` vs process-step transitions
findings.detail: process-step(s) PS-FR-003 transition into it, but its `affects` omits them.

findings.title: Flow mismatch: EX-FR-003 `affects` vs process-step transitions
findings.detail: process-step(s) PS-FR-005 transition into it, but its `affects` omits them.

findings.title: Flow mismatch: EX-FR-004 `affects` vs process-step transitions
findings.detail: process-step(s) PS-FR-006 transition into it, but its `affects` omits them.

findings.title: First-line approval segregation of duties is not covered by a control
findings.detail: PS-FR-004 requires the first-line approver to be a different analyst from the one who validated the request at PS-FR-002 — a segregation-of-duties rule. The only segregation control, CP-FR-003, covers the four-eyes check at PS-FR-006 (approver ≠ first-line approver). No control enforces the validator-≠-first-line-approver split, so that separation is asserted in step prose but uncontrolled.

findings.title: PS-FR-001 and PS-FR-009 carry no RACI assignment
findings.detail: No role records a `raci` entry for PS-FR-001 (Receive request) or PS-FR-009 (Refer for limit review). ROLE-FR-001 Front Office originates and submits the release request but has no `raci` field at all, so the intake step has no Responsible role; PS-FR-009's onward referral to Compliance and the relationship manager is likewise unassigned.

findings.title: Role prose claims accountability the RACI codes never assign
findings.detail: ROLE-FR-001, ROLE-FR-003, ROLE-FR-004 and ROLE-FR-005 each describe themselves as 'accountable' for a step, but every `raci` entry across all roles uses only R and C — no Accountable (A) is assigned for any process-step. ROLE-FR-001 Front Office carries no `raci` field despite its prose claiming accountability for the release request.

findings.title: Client-experience evidence is not traced to the process
findings.detail: Every market-trend and competitor-innovation element carries a `bearsOn` relation tying it to the steps, metrics or gaps it touches, but the three cx-benchmarks (CXB-FR-001..003) and six competitor-cx elements (CXE/CXF/CXG-FR-*) carry none. The client-experience gaps they describe — no real-time visibility, no client portal — are not linked to PS-FR-007, PS-FR-008 or the metrics, so the CX evidence sits unconnected to the process it indicts.

findings.title: Process index `docStatus` says empty but the process is fully documented
findings.detail: The Funds Release index page carries `docStatus: empty` while the process holds nine steps, six controls, four exceptions, five roles, five systems and a full CX/innovation set, and is itself `approval: approved`. The `docStatus` value contradicts the documented and approved state.

findings.title: Steps reference exceptions by ad-hoc codes that no exception carries
findings.detail: PS-FR-002, PS-FR-003 and PS-FR-005 refer in prose to exceptions as 'E-1', 'E-2' and 'E-3', but the documented exception elements are EX-FR-001..004 and carry no such short code. The prose references resolve to nothing — a reader cannot follow 'E-2' to EX-FR-002.

findings.title: Systems carry a stale `steps` frontmatter list
findings.detail: SYS-FR-001..005 each declare a `steps` id list, but the schema's system type no longer defines that relation — `process-step.systems` is now the authoritative link (it produces the reverse 'Steps' group on each system). The `steps` field on the system pages is dead frontmatter that will silently drift from the process-step links it duplicates.

findings.title: Should the incomplete-request friction be a documented gap?
findings.detail: II-FR-002 (front-office self-service portal) lists `addresses: [PG-FR-001]` — the duplicate-detection gap — but its stated benefit also says it 'cuts the incomplete or invalid requests that today bounce back from validation as exception E-1' (EX-FR-001). That incomplete-request friction has no process-gap element, so part of what II-FR-002 solves is not represented as a gap. Should it be one?

findings.title: Is PS-FR-008 meant to be a terminal step?
findings.detail: PS-FR-008 (Confirm & close) carries a single `branch` transition — to PS-FR-009 when the facility is near its limit — and no `normal` transition for the close-out path that most items take. A `branch` implies a choice, but only one arm is encoded; the ordinary 'release closed, no referral' outcome is left implicit. Should PS-FR-008's terminal path be made explicit, or is the step intended to simply end?
